executive_summary:
A Web Application Vulnerability Assessment was conducted against `http://192.168.0.102/dvwa/` using AI Red Team Harness v3. The scan completed in 29 seconds and examined 1 URL(s), 1 form(s), and 0 parameter(s).

**Overall Risk Rating: CRITICAL** (score 100.0/100)

The assessment identified **20 finding(s)**: 1 CRITICAL, 6 HIGH, 3 MEDIUM, 9 LOW, 1 INFO.

**Critical issues requiring immediate attention:**
- DVWA Module Accessible — upload

findings.title: DVWA Module Accessible — upload
findings.comparison_result: DVWA module at /vulnerabilities/upload/ returned HTTP 200. Authenticated: True. Module is active and ready for testing.
findings.description: DVWA File Upload module is accessible. Upload a PHP webshell with .php extension to achieve RCE.
findings.evidence: HTTP 200 for http://192.168.0.102/dvwa/vulnerabilities/upload/. DVWA session active: True
findings.remediation: DVWA is intentionally vulnerable. Use only in isolated lab environments. Never expose DVWA to the internet or production networks.
reproduction_steps: 1. Navigate to http://192.168.0.102/dvwa/vulnerabilities/upload/
2. Set DVWA Security Level to Low in /security.php
3. DVWA File Upload module is accessible. Upload a PHP webshell with .php extension to achieve RCE.
findings.validation_logic: DVWA-specific module detected and accessible. DVWA modules are designed to be exploitable — treat all findings as CONFIRMED in the context of authorized lab testing.
findings.exploitability: DVWA File Upload module is accessible. Upload a PHP webshell with .php extension to achieve RCE.

findings.title: DVWA Module Accessible — sqli
findings.comparison_result: DVWA module at /vulnerabilities/sqli/ returned HTTP 200. Authenticated: True. Module is active and ready for testing.
findings.description: DVWA SQL Injection module is accessible. Submit 1' OR '1'='1 or 1 UNION SELECT 1,2-- in the ID field.
findings.evidence: HTTP 200 for http://192.168.0.102/dvwa/vulnerabilities/sqli/. DVWA session active: True
findings.remediation: DVWA is intentionally vulnerable. Use only in isolated lab environments. Never expose DVWA to the internet or production networks.
reproduction_steps: 1. Navigate to http://192.168.0.102/dvwa/vulnerabilities/sqli/
2. Set DVWA Security Level to Low in /security.php
3. DVWA SQL Injection module is accessible. Submit 1' OR '1'='1 or 1 UNION SELECT 1,2-- in the ID field.
findings.validation_logic: DVWA-specific module detected and accessible. DVWA modules are designed to be exploitable — treat all findings as CONFIRMED in the context of authorized lab testing.
findings.exploitability: DVWA SQL Injection module is accessible. Submit 1' OR '1'='1 or 1 UNION SELECT 1,2-- in the ID field.

findings.title: Application Served Over HTTP (No TLS)
findings.comparison_result: Engine detection: URL scheme is 'http': http://192.168.0.102/dvwa/
findings.description: The application is served over plain HTTP. All traffic (including credentials and session tokens) is transmitted in cleartext.
findings.remediation: Enforce HTTPS for all endpoints. Obtain a TLS certificate and redirect all HTTP traffic to HTTPS. Configure HSTS.
reproduction_steps: 1. Navigate to endpoint: http://192.168.0.102/dvwa/
2. Identify parameter:
3. Send the following test payload (see Raw Request).
4. Observe response for: URL scheme is 'http': http://192.168.0.102/dvwa/
5. Compare against baseline to confirm anomaly.
6. Title: Application Served Over HTTP (No TLS)
findings.validation_logic: Confidence 100% based on engine detection. Evidence: URL scheme is 'http': http://192.168.0.102/dvwa/
findings.exploitability: Evidence supports exploitability as described.

findings.title: Accessible Admin/Debug Endpoint — /phpinfo
findings.comparison_result: Engine detection: HTTP 200 for http://192.168.0.102/dvwa/phpinfo
findings.description: Admin/debug endpoint '/phpinfo' returned HTTP 200. It is accessible without authentication.
findings.remediation: Remove or restrict all debug and admin endpoints in production. Use IP allowlisting or VPN for administrative interfaces. Disable framework debug modes (debug=False, RAILS_ENV=production).

2. Set DVWA Security Level to Low in /security.php

findings.title: [POTENTIAL ISSUE — MANUAL VALIDATION REQUIRED] Missing Content-Security-Policy Header
findings.comparison_result: Baseline: header '' absent from server response. No exploit demonstrated — classified as configuration gap.
findings.description: No Content-Security-Policy header is present. CSP provides an important additional layer of defence against XSS.
findings.evidence: CSP header absent from server response
findings.remediation: Define a strict CSP. Start with default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; and tighten progressively.
reproduction_steps: 1. Navigate to endpoint: http://192.168.0.102/dvwa/
2. Identify parameter:
3. Send the following test payload (see Raw Request).
4. Observe response for: CSP header absent from server response
5. Compare against baseline to confirm anomaly.
6. Title: Missing Content-Security-Policy Header
findings.validation_logic: Per Rule 7: missing security headers are configuration issues, not directly exploitable vulnerabilities. Severity capped unless an exploit chain is demonstrated. Header '' absence represents a legitimate risk and is not downgraded.
findings.exploitability: Not directly exploitable. Requires additional attacker conditions (e.g., MITM, social engineering, co-located vulnerability) to leverage.

findings.title: [POTENTIAL ISSUE — MANUAL VALIDATION REQUIRED] Missing X-Frame-Options Header
findings.comparison_result: Baseline: header 'x-frame-options' absent from server response. No exploit demonstrated — classified as configuration gap.
findings.description: Absent X-Frame-Options allows the page to be embedded in iframes (clickjacking risk).
findings.remediation: Add: X-Frame-Options: DENY or SAMEORIGIN. Prefer CSP frame-ancestors directive.
reproduction_steps: 1. Navigate to endpoint: http://192.168.0.102/dvwa/
2. Identify parameter: x-frame-options
3. Send the following test payload (see Raw Request).
4. Observe response for: Header 'x-frame-options' not present in response
5. Compare against baseline to confirm anomaly.
6. Title: Missing X-Frame-Options Header
findings.validation_logic: Per Rule 7: missing security headers are configuration issues, not directly exploitable vulnerabilities. Severity capped unless an exploit chain is demonstrated. Header 'x-frame-options' absence represents a legitimate risk and is not downgraded.
findings.exploitability: Not directly exploitable. Requires additional attacker conditions (e.g., MITM, social engineering, co-located vulnerability) to leverage.

findings.title: [POTENTIAL ISSUE — MANUAL VALIDATION REQUIRED] Missing CSRF Token — http://192.168.0.102/dvwa/login.php
findings.comparison_result: Baseline: form inputs present. Observation: no CSRF token field found in ['username', 'password', 'Login']. Exploit requires: tokenless POST succeeds with state change on victim session.
findings.description: A POST form was found without any detectable CSRF token field. UNVERIFIED — confirmation requires a tokenless submission to succeed on a victim session context.
findings.evidence: POST form at 'http://192.168.0.102/dvwa/login.php' has no CSRF token field. Fields: ['username', 'password', 'Login']
findings.remediation: Add a cryptographically random CSRF token to every state-changing form. Validate the token server-side on every POST/PUT/DELETE request. Use the SameSite=Strict or SameSite=Lax cookie attribute.
reproduction_steps: 1. Navigate to endpoint: http://192.168.0.102/dvwa/login.php
2. Identify parameter:
3. Send the following test payload (see Raw Request).
4. Observe response for: POST form at 'http://192.168.0.102/dvwa/login.php' has no CSRF token field. Fields: ['username', 'password', 'Login']
5. Compare against baseline to confirm anomaly.
6. Title: Missing CSRF Token — http://192.168.0.102/dvwa/login.php
findings.validation_logic: Confidence 50% based on engine detection. Evidence: POST form at 'http://192.168.0.102/dvwa/login.php' has no CSRF token field. Fields: ['username', 'password', 'Login']
findings.exploitability: Requires manual verification to assess full impact scope.

findings.title: [POTENTIAL ISSUE — MANUAL VALIDATION REQUIRED] DVWA Module Accessible — xss_s
findings.comparison_result: DVWA module at /vulnerabilities/xss_s/ returned HTTP 200. Authenticated: True. Module is active and ready for testing.
findings.description: DVWA Stored XSS module is accessible. Store a persistent XSS payload in the message board.
findings.evidence: HTTP 200 for http://192.168.0.102/dvwa/vulnerabilities/xss_s/. DVWA session active: True
findings.remediation: DVWA is intentionally vulnerable. Use only in isolated lab environments. Never expose DVWA to the internet or production networks.
findings.raw_response_excerpt: HTTP 200 for http://192.168.0.102/dvwa/vulnerabilities/xss_s/. DVWA session active: True
reproduction_steps: 1. Send GET/POST to http://192.168.0.102/dvwa/vulnerabilities/xss_s/ with =
2. Inspect response HTML for unencoded tag.
3. Load in browser and verify onerror fires before classifying as XSS.
findings.validation_logic: Marker was reflected in the HTML body context. HTML body reflection requires a full tag injection to execute JavaScript. The test marker used (<xssXXX>) does not contain executable JavaScript. Manual testing with a real payload required to confirm execution.
findings.exploitability: LOW — HTML body reflection. Test with to determine if tags survive server-side sanitisation.

findings.title: [POTENTIAL ISSUE — MANUAL VALIDATION REQUIRED] Missing X-Content-Type-Options Header
findings.comparison_result: Baseline: header 'x-content-type-options' absent from server response. No exploit demonstrated — classified as configuration gap.
findings.description: Without nosniff, browsers may MIME-sniff responses, enabling content injection.
findings.remediation: Add: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
reproduction_steps: 1. Navigate to endpoint: http://192.168.0.102/dvwa/
2. Identify parameter: x-content-type-options
3. Send the following test payload (see Raw Request).
4. Observe response for: Header 'x-content-type-options' not present in response
5. Compare against baseline to confirm anomaly.
6. Title: Missing X-Content-Type-Options Header
findings.validation_logic: Per Rule 7: missing security headers are configuration issues, not directly exploitable vulnerabilities. Severity capped unless an exploit chain is demonstrated. Header 'x-content-type-options' absence represents a legitimate risk and is not downgraded.
findings.exploitability: Not directly exploitable. Requires additional attacker conditions (e.g., MITM, social engineering, co-located vulnerability) to leverage.

findings.title: Server Version Disclosure in Header
findings.comparison_result: Engine detection: Server: Apache/2.2.8 (Ubuntu) DAV/2
findings.description: The 'Server' header discloses server software and version: 'Apache/2.2.8 (Ubuntu) DAV/2'. This aids fingerprinting for targeted attacks.
findings.remediation: Configure the server to emit a generic 'Server' header or omit it entirely.
reproduction_steps: 1. Navigate to endpoint: http://192.168.0.102/dvwa/
2. Identify parameter: Server
3. Send the following test payload (see Raw Request).
4. Observe response for: Server: Apache/2.2.8 (Ubuntu) DAV/2
5. Compare against baseline to confirm anomaly.
6. Title: Server Version Disclosure in Header
findings.validation_logic: Confidence 95% based on engine detection. Evidence: Server: Apache/2.2.8 (Ubuntu) DAV/2
findings.exploitability: Evidence supports exploitability as described.

findings.title: Technology Disclosure via X-Powered-By Header
findings.comparison_result: Engine detection: X-Powered-By: PHP/5.2.4-2ubuntu5.10
findings.description: 'X-Powered-By: PHP/5.2.4-2ubuntu5.10' discloses the application framework/runtime version.
findings.remediation: Remove or suppress the X-Powered-By header in server/framework configuration.
reproduction_steps: 1. Navigate to endpoint: http://192.168.0.102/dvwa/
2. Identify parameter: X-Powered-By
3. Send the following test payload (see Raw Request).
4. Observe response for: X-Powered-By: PHP/5.2.4-2ubuntu5.10
5. Compare against baseline to confirm anomaly.
6. Title: Technology Disclosure via X-Powered-By Header
findings.validation_logic: Confidence 95% based on engine detection. Evidence: X-Powered-By: PHP/5.2.4-2ubuntu5.10
findings.exploitability: Evidence supports exploitability as described.

findings.title: [POTENTIAL ISSUE — MANUAL VALIDATION REQUIRED] Missing Referrer-Policy Header
findings.comparison_result: Baseline: header 'referrer-policy' absent from server response. No exploit demonstrated — classified as configuration gap.
findings.description: No Referrer-Policy means browsers use their default, potentially leaking URLs.
findings.remediation: Add: Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin or no-referrer
reproduction_steps: 1. Navigate to endpoint: http://192.168.0.102/dvwa/
2. Identify parameter: referrer-policy
3. Send the following test payload (see Raw Request).

findings.validation_logic: Per Rule 7: missing security headers are configuration issues, not directly exploitable vulnerabilities. Severity capped unless an exploit chain is demonstrated. Header 'referrer-policy' absence represents a legitimate risk and is not downgraded.
findings.exploitability: Not directly exploitable. Requires additional attacker conditions (e.g., MITM, social engineering, co-located vulnerability) to leverage.

findings.title: [POTENTIAL ISSUE — MANUAL VALIDATION REQUIRED] Missing Permissions-Policy Header
findings.comparison_result: Baseline: header 'permissions-policy' absent from server response. No exploit demonstrated — classified as configuration gap.
findings.description: No Permissions-Policy leaves browser features (camera, geolocation, mic) unrestricted.
findings.remediation: Add: Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=()

findings.exploitability: Not directly exploitable. Requires additional attacker conditions (e.g., MITM, social engineering, co-located vulnerability) to leverage.

findings.title: [POTENTIAL ISSUE — MANUAL VALIDATION REQUIRED] Missing X-XSS-Protection Header
findings.comparison_result: Header 'x-xss-protection' not present
findings.description: X-XSS-Protection is deprecated in modern browsers but its absence may affect older browser support.
findings.remediation: Set X-XSS-Protection: 0 (to disable the buggy XSS auditor) and rely on CSP instead.
reproduction_steps: 1. Send GET/POST to http://192.168.0.102/dvwa/ with x-xss-protection=
2. Inspect response HTML for unencoded tag.
3. Load in browser and verify onerror fires before classifying as XSS.
findings.validation_logic: Marker was reflected in the HTML body context. HTML body reflection requires a full tag injection to execute JavaScript. The test marker used (<xssXXX>) does not contain executable JavaScript. Manual testing with a real payload required to confirm execution.
findings.exploitability: LOW — HTML body reflection. Test with to determine if tags survive server-side sanitisation.

Disallow: /
findings.remediation: Review robots.txt for paths that reveal internal application structure. Do not rely on robots.txt to hide sensitive endpoints.

remediation_roadmap.generic_advice: Enforce server-side access controls on every endpoint. Implement least-privilege and deny-by-default policies.

findings.title: Application Served Over HTTP (No TLS)
findings.remediation: Enforce HTTPS for all endpoints. Obtain a TLS certificate and redirect all HTTP traffic to HTTPS. Configure HSTS.

remediation_roadmap.generic_advice: Enforce TLS 1.2+ with HSTS. Rotate secrets regularly. Use strong, well-reviewed cryptographic algorithms.

findings.title: Accessible Admin/Debug Endpoint — /phpinfo
findings.remediation: Remove or restrict all debug and admin endpoints in production. Use IP allowlisting or VPN for administrative interfaces. Disable framework debug modes (debug=False, RAILS_ENV=production).

findings.title: Accessible Admin/Debug Endpoint — /docs
findings.remediation: Remove or restrict all debug and admin endpoints in production. Use IP allowlisting or VPN for administrative interfaces. Disable framework debug modes (debug=False, RAILS_ENV=production).

findings.title: Accessible Admin/Debug Endpoint — /docs/
findings.remediation: Remove or restrict all debug and admin endpoints in production. Use IP allowlisting or VPN for administrative interfaces. Disable framework debug modes (debug=False, RAILS_ENV=production).

findings.title: [POTENTIAL ISSUE — MANUAL VALIDATION REQUIRED] Missing X-Frame-Options Header
findings.remediation: Add: X-Frame-Options: DENY or SAMEORIGIN. Prefer CSP frame-ancestors directive.

findings.title: [POTENTIAL ISSUE — MANUAL VALIDATION REQUIRED] Sensitive File Exposed — /phpinfo.php
findings.remediation: Block direct access to '/phpinfo.php' via web server configuration. Move sensitive files outside the web root. Audit deployment scripts to prevent secrets from being committed.

findings.title: [POTENTIAL ISSUE — MANUAL VALIDATION REQUIRED] Missing X-Content-Type-Options Header
findings.remediation: Add: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff

findings.title: [POTENTIAL ISSUE — MANUAL VALIDATION REQUIRED] Missing Referrer-Policy Header
findings.remediation: Add: Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin or no-referrer

findings.title: [POTENTIAL ISSUE — MANUAL VALIDATION REQUIRED] Missing Permissions-Policy Header
findings.remediation: Add: Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=()

findings.title: [POTENTIAL ISSUE — MANUAL VALIDATION REQUIRED] Missing X-XSS-Protection Header
findings.remediation: Set X-XSS-Protection: 0 (to disable the buggy XSS auditor) and rely on CSP instead.

findings.title: [POTENTIAL ISSUE — MANUAL VALIDATION REQUIRED] robots.txt Accessible — /robots.txt
findings.remediation: Review robots.txt for paths that reveal internal application structure. Do not rely on robots.txt to hide sensitive endpoints.

remediation_roadmap.generic_advice: Harden server configuration, remove debug/default pages, set security headers (CSP, X-Frame-Options, HSTS, etc.).

findings.title: [POTENTIAL ISSUE — MANUAL VALIDATION REQUIRED] Missing Content-Security-Policy Header
findings.remediation: Define a strict CSP. Start with default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; and tighten progressively.

remediation_roadmap.generic_advice: Use parameterised queries / prepared statements. Apply strict input validation and output encoding.

findings.title: [POTENTIAL ISSUE — MANUAL VALIDATION REQUIRED] Missing CSRF Token — http://192.168.0.102/dvwa/login.php
findings.remediation: Add a cryptographically random CSRF token to every state-changing form. Validate the token server-side on every POST/PUT/DELETE request. Use the SameSite=Strict or SameSite=Lax cookie attribute.

remediation_roadmap.generic_advice: Enforce server-side access controls on every endpoint. Implement least-privilege and deny-by-default policies.

findings.title: Server Version Disclosure in Header
findings.remediation: Configure the server to emit a generic 'Server' header or omit it entirely.

findings.title: Technology Disclosure via X-Powered-By Header
findings.remediation: Remove or suppress the X-Powered-By header in server/framework configuration.

remediation_roadmap.generic_advice: Implement MFA, enforce strong password policies, use secure session management and token rotation.
